sync.title: Cloud Sync
sync.subtitle: Sync profiles across your devices. Only encrypted ciphertext leaves this machine.
sync.disabledNotice: Sync is disabled — no endpoint configured. Nothing is uploaded.
sync.passphraseLabel: Sync passphrase (encrypts everything — share it across your devices)
sync.passphrasePlaceholderConfigured: Re-enter to unlock, or set a new one to re-key
sync.passphraseWarning: Zero-knowledge: this passphrase never leaves your device. If you lose it, synced data can't be recovered. Changing it re-keys (old remote data becomes unreadable).
sync.msgConfiguredUnlocked: Sync configured and unlocked for this session.
sync.msgSavedEnterPassphrase: Saved. Enter your sync passphrase to unlock.
sync.errUnlockCheckPassphrase: Could not unlock — check your passphrase.
sync.errLockedEnterPassphrase: Sync is locked — enter your passphrase first.

dbEncryption.title: Database Encryption
dbEncryption.subtitle: Encrypt the entire workspace database with your workspace password.
dbEncryption.scopeBefore: When the app is closed, the database is stored only as encrypted ciphertext that a file reader cannot open. While the app is
dbEncryption.scopeAfter: , a working copy is decrypted on disk so the app can use it — so this protects a stolen or copied drive, not a live, running machine.
dbEncryption.recommendedBefore: This is the most complete way to protect
dbEncryption.recommendedAfter: , 2FA seeds, saved cookies and account data at rest — it encrypts every field, not just some. Make a workspace backup first, then turn it on below.
dbEncryption.enabledNotice: On next launch you'll be asked to unlock the database with your workspace password before the app opens.
dbEncryption.readFirstBefore: Encryption uses your workspace password as the key. If you forget it, your data
dbEncryption.readFirstAfter: — there is no backdoor and no reset. Make a workspace backup before enabling.
dbEncryption.ackUnrecoverable: I understand that if I lose this password, the encrypted database is permanently unrecoverable.
dbEncryption.disableIntro: Turning encryption off decrypts the database back to plaintext on this device. Enter your workspace password to confirm.
dbEncryption.errConfirmRisk: Please confirm you understand the recovery risk.
dbEncryption.errEnterPasswordConfirm: Enter your workspace password to confirm.
dbEncryption.msgEnabled: Database encryption is on. The database file is now encrypted whenever the app is closed.
dbEncryption.msgDisabled: Database encryption is off. The database is stored as plaintext again.

workspaceBackup.title: Workspace Backup & Restore
workspaceBackup.subtitle: Export your whole workspace (database + settings) as one encrypted file.
workspaceBackup.backupPasswordHint: The backup is encrypted with this password — store it somewhere safe; it can't be recovered.
workspaceBackup.restorePasswordPlaceholder: Password used for the backup file
workspaceBackup.confirmRestoreLabel: I understand this replaces my current workspace with the backup's contents.
workspaceBackup.footerNote: A backup captures profiles, settings and credentials as they are in the database — not the on-disk browser caches. The file is verified before any restore overwrites your current data, so a wrong password or corrupted file fails safely.
workspaceBackup.errPasswordMin: Choose a backup password of at least 6 characters.
workspaceBackup.errEnterPassword: Enter the password for the backup file.
workspaceBackup.errConfirmRestore: Please confirm — restoring replaces your current workspace.
workspaceBackup.msgRestored: Workspace restored. Restart the app to make sure everything reloads cleanly.

branding.footerCardDesc: Shown at the bottom of every page. Use {year} for the current year; links open in your browser.
branding.savedReopens: Saved · reopens on next page load
branding.onlySuperFooter: Only the Super Admin can change the footer.
branding.staySignedInTitle: Stay signed in on this device
branding.staySignedInDesc: Skip the password on every restart. The credential is encrypted to your Windows account (DPAPI).
branding.osEncryptionUnavailable: OS encryption isn't available on this device, so auto sign-in can't be enabled here.
branding.autoSignInOnHint: This device will open the workspace without asking for the password. Turn it off to require the password again on the next start.
branding.autoSignInOffHintQuote: “Keep me signed in on this device”
branding.autoSignInOffHintAfter: on the sign-in screen to enable it. Signing out always turns it off.
branding.autofillDesc: The in-page identity widget shown on signup forms in launched Chromium profiles.
branding.autofillEnabled: Enabled — the Data Vault widget is injected into launched profiles.
branding.autofillDisabled: Disabled — no autofill widget is injected.
branding.autofillOnlyOwner: Only the Owner or Super Admin can change this.
